pages.name: Beyond the Investigation — Greyhawk City Index

text.content:
Sandbox layer for the Free City of Greyhawk. The party reaches Greyhawk only via Phase 5's Vector A. Greyhawk is the campaign's largest city and the most cosmopolitan; downtime here is the broadest of any sandbox in the module. Anchors are scoped lightly because the party will likely spend less time in Greyhawk than in Loftwick or Rel Astra during the current campaign — but if the party returns in a sequel campaign, this layer can be expanded.

What's in this journal

  • Per-PC anchors — Greyhawk contacts, scoped to a single primary anchor each (Greyhawk is the city the party knows least, so anchors are introductions rather than long-standing relationships).
  • Civic calendar — Greyhawk's civic and faction rhythms; the city's three quarter-festivals and its weekly council schedule.
  • Carousing & information-gathering — d10 tables for the Foreign Quarter, the Garden Quarter, and the High Quay.
  • Crafting, training, recuperation — what Greyhawk uniquely offers (rare arcana, fight pits, exotic services).
  • Sub-team mini-quests — four short evening scenes.

How Greyhawk City differs from the other three cities

  • Greyhawk is large. 50,000+ souls in normal years; far more in festival weeks. Districts work like small cities; the Foreign Quarter alone has more taverns than Loftwick has streets. PCs cannot expect to be remembered between visits unless they make a relationship.
  • The factions are real. The Thieves' Guild, the Beggars' Union, the various trading houses, the City Watch, and a half-dozen private interests all maintain their own networks and (broadly) tolerate one another. The party's mere presence in Greyhawk is being noted by at least three of these networks; the anchors below help the party manage which.
  • The conspiracy is here too. Phase 5's Vector A explicitly puts the party on the conspiracy's Greyhawk node (the Brass Crow counting-house, V. Korre's hand-altered name in Edrik Vone's ledger). Sandbox-layer activity in Greyhawk should be low-key; visible carousing draws attention the party may not want.

The Ash Tree in the Foreign Quarter. Brisa keeps her herb-stall under it on market days.

Skarrel. Cam's anchor in Greyhawk City.

Cam — the halfling rogue

Anchor location: The Folded Cup, a small fence-and-pawn operation tucked into a Foreign Quarter alley off Tinker's Way. Half a block from the Brass Crow counting-house. Run primarily for halflings and other Foreign Quarter regulars.

Anchor NPC: Skarrel, a halfling fence (no known surname; Greyhawk halflings often go by single names) of indeterminate age (probably forties), originally from Elmshire south of Greyhawk, settled in the city eighteen years ago. Recognizes Cam as a fellow halfling cant-speaker on first meeting. Hesp Olfair (Cam's Hardby anchor) and Rinya Dane (Cam's Rel Astra anchor) both know Skarrel professionally.

What he offers: 55-65% fence-rate buy-and-sell. The Foreign Quarter's underground network — courier routes, safe-houses, the names of three discreet receivers who handle items the conspiracy might recognize. A back room with halfling-comfortable furniture and the day's news in three languages.

What he needs (if Cam offers): Skarrel knows that the Brass Crow counting-house has been receiving foreign packets that, by his network's reading, do not pass through any Greyhawk fence-channel he recognizes. He is professionally curious and would like Cam, if Cam learns anything in the course of the party's Vector A investigation, to share what he can. (This bridges sandbox to investigation; treat it as Skarrel asking a favor for shared information rather than as a separate mini-quest.)

Kell Marrow. Elle's anchor in Greyhawk City.

Elle — the halfling monk

Anchor location: The Gravestone, a public sporting yard in the Garden Quarter — a covered ring with raised seating, used for friendly sparring matches three afternoons a week and for the occasional public competition during festivals.

Anchor NPC: Kell Marrow, a retired pugilist (human, Greyhawk-born, late fifties, broad-shouldered, walks with a limp from an old fight), runs the Gravestone with his daughter Tessa. Welcomes traveling martial practitioners; halflings are uncommon enough at the yard that Elle is genuinely a curiosity rather than a routine visitor.

What he offers: Sparring (any afternoon the yard is open). The Gravestone's small library of fight-records — useful as cultural reference for Elle's tradition. A cup of Greyhawk small-beer in the office between rounds. Information on which Greyhawk martial styles are currently in fashion and which trainers are accepting students.

What he needs (if Elle offers): A young human pugilist Kell has been training for six months has hit a plateau. Kell suspects the boy is over-relying on his reach. He would like Elle to spar a few rounds — "a halfling teaches what reach hides." (Mini-quest 2.)

Brisa Wood. Kitty's anchor in Greyhawk City.

Kitty — the chthonic tiefling druid

Anchor location: The Ash Tree, an unusual surviving tree at the eastern edge of the Foreign Quarter, in a small lot the city has not built on for forty years. A single ash-tree of indeterminate age, considered locally a small marker.

Anchor NPC: Brisa Wood, a hedge-witch (human, Flan-Suel-mixed, mid-fifties, weathered, dresses in plain Greyhawk linen with a small herb-bundle worn at the throat). Keeps a tiny herb-stall under the Ash Tree on market days; lives in two rooms a block away. Recognizes Kitty's chthonic ancestry on first meeting and is, like Mother Felun and Helka Fenn, calm about it.

What she offers: Conversation under the tree. Greyhawk-specific herbal knowledge (the city has plants that grow nowhere else in the campaign — small adaptations to centuries of urban presence). The names of two other hedge-practitioners in the city who Brisa trusts. A small offering-bowl at the Ash Tree's roots that Kitty may use without explanation.

What she needs (if Kitty offers): The Ash Tree itself has been losing leaves on its eastern side for the last three weeks. The cause is unclear. Brisa would like Kitty's reading — Brisa has lived under this tree for thirty years and has not seen this happen before. (Mini-quest 3.)

Sergeant Brel Vandros. Gianni's anchor in Greyhawk City.

Gianni — the human ranger

Anchor location: The Foreign Quarter watch station, a small City Watch post near the eastern gate of the Foreign Quarter. Less formal than Hardby's or Rel Astra's; the Foreign Quarter watch is famously practical.

Anchor NPC: Sergeant Brel Vandros, a Greyhawk City Watch sergeant (human, Greyhawk-born, late forties, weathered, lost two fingers of his right hand to an old fight; left-handed since), runs the Foreign Quarter watch's day-shift roster. Greyhawk's faction politics are intricate; Vandros is faction-neutral and respects competence.

What he offers: The Foreign Quarter watch's incident log (does not include conspiracy material; includes routine bandit, smuggler, and tavern-fight reports). Information on which Greyhawk roads are running, which gates are seeing unusual traffic, and which faction-runners have been visible this week. The names of two reliable Foreign Quarter informers if Gianni needs to make a one-time discreet inquiry.

What he needs (if Gianni offers): A small smuggling route operating through the eastern gate has been moving items the Foreign Quarter watch cannot identify — small wrapped packages, weight inconsistent with normal contraband. Vandros would like Gianni's eye on the route's pattern, off the record. (Mini-quest 4.)

Iren Velash. Selvara's anchor in Greyhawk City.

Selvara — the human sorcerer

Anchor location: Iren Velash's manuscript shop, in the Garden Quarter. (Already established in Phase 5 Vector A as a Caelith-aligned contact and Selvara's natural professional anchor.)

Anchor NPC: Iren Velash, half-Suel woman, mid-fifties, manuscript dealer. Quiet, honest, not friendly. Selvara approaches her under Caelith's introduction (the phrase "the seventh question of the third volume"). Once Iren has confirmed Selvara's standing, she becomes a downtime anchor as well as an investigation contact.

What she offers: Manuscript readings (the campaign's deepest such service in any city). Access to her private back-shelf, which contains a half-dozen folio volumes Magister Vaden of Rel Astra would pay handsomely to consult. A small reading desk at the back of the shop where Selvara may spend an entire day quietly.

What she needs (if Selvara offers): Iren has acquired, via a recent estate-clearance, a small bundle of correspondence she does not have the time or specialty to read carefully. The correspondence is in pre-Suel script and Selvara's recent training under Magister Vaden makes her uniquely equipped. Iren will pay 15 gp for a careful summary. (Mini-quest 5; takes 2-3 days but the work is quiet and atmospheric.)

Alicia — the warlock

Greyhawk does not have a direct Pact-of-the-Blade anchor for Alicia; her tradition is small enough that few cities maintain dedicated specialists outside Rel Astra. Alicia's downtime in Greyhawk is best spent at Iren Velash's shop as a secondary visitor (Iren reads warlock signs and respects them, though her specialty is manuscripts rather than pacts), at the Garden Quarter bath-houses (atmospheric and restorative), or in quiet correspondence with Trina via the Foreign Quarter post.

If the party stays in Greyhawk for an extended period (more than five days), Iren can introduce Alicia to a small private fellowship of Greyhawk warlocks who meet irregularly at a Garden Quarter wine-merchant's back room. The fellowship is not a major plot thread; it is a quiet circle of practitioners who share a pact-tradition tea once a season.

pages.name: Civic Calendar — What's Happening in Greyhawk This Week

text.content:
The Greyhawk year

Same Greyhawk twelve-month calendar. Greyhawk City's festival emphasis differs from all three other campaign cities:

  • Needfest — quietly observed; the Foreign Quarter is at half-pace, the rest of the city continues. Most temples hold modest observances.
  • Growfest — the city's large public festival of the year. Three days of street-fairs, public games at the Gravestone (Elle's anchor), trade-quarter feasts. The party will find Greyhawk at its loudest.
  • Richfest — moderate local observance. Council holds public lectures; the great trading houses host feasts.
  • Brewfest — major; the Foreign Quarter's harvest-festival is the largest in the city. Pickpockets are at peak; Skarrel's fence-traffic is at peak.

The Greyhawk week

DayWhat's openNotes
StardayMarkets (full); Council not in session; entertainments fullThe week's largest market day. The Foreign Quarter's main square is loud from sunrise.
SundayTemples (full); markets (small); commerce at halfReligious observance day. Greyhawk has more temples than the campaign's other cities combined; choose by tradition.
MoondayCouncil in session; markets (small); entertainments fullCouncil session day. Decisions affecting the Foreign Quarter are often discussed in the morning sessions.
GodsdayCouncil in session; markets (full); entertainments fullSecond main market day.
WaterdayMarkets (small); Council not in session; entertainments fullThe Gravestone holds open-sparring afternoons. Iren Velash's shop is at its quietest.
EarthdayMarkets (small); Council committee day; entertainments fullTrade-quarter guild meetings.
FreedayMarkets (medium); Council not in session; entertainments at peakThe week's largest entertainment night. Pickpockets and watch are both busy.

Local events the party may run into

  • The Brass Crow's morning (any day at the eleventh bell; under Phase 5 Vector A). Public; the registry the party is investigating.
  • Iren Velash's shop hours (any day except Sunday, second to seventh bell; Selvara's anchor).
  • The Gravestone open-sparring (Waterday afternoons, Garden Quarter; Elle's anchor).
  • The Folded Cup back room (any evening after the seventh bell; Cam's anchor).
  • The Ash Tree (any market day, second to fifth bell; Kitty's anchor — Brisa keeps her stall).
  • The Foreign Quarter watch station (Sergeant Vandros's day-shift roster, Starday through Earthday, sixth bell to second bell of the next morning; Gianni's anchor).

An evening at a Foreign Quarter tavern (Brass Crow excluded)

Cost: 5-8 sp passable; 1 gp comfortable; 5-10 gp memorable. Persuasion or Insight DC 12. On success, roll d10.

d10What you heard
1An Aerdy traveler is in the Foreign Quarter on a quiet errand he will not discuss. (Routine; could be Haskur Vandrell, could be three other Aerdy travelers; do not over-cue.)
2The Brass Crow counting-house has been receiving more foreign post than usual. Skarrel's network has noticed. (Sandbox-to-investigation bridge for Cam.)
3A Greyhawk Thieves' Guild representative has been seen drinking at the same tavern three nights running. He is recruiting; he is also drunk; he is not subtle.
4A retired Greyhawk City Watch lieutenant is at the next table arguing with his nephew about whether the City Watch's pension is fair. (Routine.)
5An Aerdy poet is in town. (Yes — possibly the same poet from the Hardby and Rel Astra carousing tables. The poet's circuit is established.)
6A halfling at the bar offers Cam a small folded note: a name and an address. Cam recognizes the writing as Skarrel's. The note is a referral to a Garden Quarter wine-merchant Skarrel trusts.
7The Foreign Quarter has lost three couriers to unidentified causes in the last fortnight. The City Watch is investigating; nobody has connected the cases yet.
8A traveling Suel scholar is staying at the Quarter's largest inn. She is the same scholar mentioned in the Rel Astra Brass Sextant table — she is on a circuit. (Selvara may meet her in either city; introductions transfer.)
9A man with a gait that is, in the speaker's words, "not quite right" was seen at the Brass Crow yesterday. (Phase 5 Vector A: this is the younger man with Haskur. Reinforces the ongoing investigation.)
10The tavern's serving-girl tells the speaker, quietly, that the Foreign Quarter has more eyes on it this season than last. She does not elaborate. She is correct.

An afternoon at the Garden Quarter (manuscript shops, bath-houses, Iren Velash)

Cost: free for browsing; 1-3 gp for a bath-house afternoon; manuscripts vary. Investigation or Persuasion DC 12. On success, roll d10.

d10What you saw or learned
1A Garden Quarter bookbinder has acquired a small folio of pre-Aerdy paleography. Iren Velash will hear about it within the day; Selvara may approach the bookbinder first. (Atmosphere, not investigation.)
2A traveling musician is performing in a Garden Quarter wine-merchant's back room nightly this week. Free entry; expects tips.
3The Garden Quarter's largest bath-house is hosting a small public competition for "the most bored cat in Greyhawk." Three cats have been entered. Kitty may attend without explanation.
4Iren Velash mentions, in passing, that her supplier of dyed-vellum has not delivered this week. (Routine; the supplier's mule went lame.)
5A retired Greyhawk warlock is taking tea at a Garden Quarter merchant's. Iren acknowledges him with a nod. (Alicia's introduction to the small Greyhawk warlock fellowship may begin here.)
6A pickpocket is working the Garden Quarter's main street, badly. The City Watch will catch him within the hour.
7Iren Velash receives a sealed letter at her shop's door. She reads it without comment and adds it to a small stack on her desk. (Routine correspondence.)
8A Greyhawk-Foreign-Quarter scholar is publicly debating a Garden Quarter scholar over the merits of pre-Aerdy versus post-Aerdy paleographic reform. The argument will not resolve.
9A fortune-teller at the bath-house's lobby offers a free reading. The reading is generic and has no in-game effect; the fortune-teller is honest about being entertainment.
10A small Garden Quarter wine-merchant offers Selvara a complimentary glass of his best Aerdy red, in honor of her recent acquaintance with Iren. Iren does not promote her clients; the wine-merchant is paying attention.

A morning at the High Quay (private courier archive, harbor-side, Edrik Vone)

Cost: free for observation; bribes apply where Vone is involved. Investigation or Persuasion DC 13. On success, roll d10.

d10What you saw or learned
1A small fishing-skiff has just brought in the morning catch. The fishmongers are auctioning publicly.
2Edrik Vone is open today. (Phase 5 Vector A.)
3A Greyhawk merchant is openly arguing with a customs official over a small tariff. The argument is procedurally unhelpful.
4A retired Greyhawk City Watch officer is taking morning coffee at the small quay-side cafe. He recognizes Sergeant Vandros's name. (If Gianni mentions it, the retired officer will share two pieces of useful Foreign Quarter history.)
5A halfling traveler at the quay is selling a small Aerdy silver brooch. (Possibly the same brooch motif from Hardby's market table; possibly different. Cam may inspect.)
6A Greyhawk dock-worker has spotted, in his own words, "a man whose feet were on the wrong way around" at the Brass Crow's loading bay yesterday. The dock-worker is drunk; he saw something. (Phase 5: this corroborates the younger man's gait. Do not over-cue.)
7A small Greyhawk-licensed coastal cog named the Black Iron sailed to Saltmarsh yesterday. Routine.
8The Foreign Quarter watch has been asking quay-side workers about smuggling routes. (Sergeant Vandros's investigation, mini-quest 4.)
9A small Greyhawk-City customs official is taking bribes openly at the quay. The City Watch knows; the bribes are below the threshold of action. (Atmosphere.)
10A Greyhawk-Foreign-Quarter halfling courier is delivering a sealed packet to the Brass Crow. (Phase 5 Vector A: this is the conspiracy's local node operating in plain sight. The halfling is innocent; he is paid by the packet.)

Crafting and gear

ServiceProviderCost / TimeNotes
Mundane weapon repairForeign Quarter and Garden Quarter smiths (multiple)5 sp - 1 gp / 1 dayGreyhawk pricing is competitive; many shops, varying quality.
Custom weapon commissionMaster Hael, Garden Quarter (referred via the Gravestone or via Iren Velash)full PHB price + 25% / 5-10 daysGreyhawk's competition keeps prices reasonable.
Exotic weapon commissionMaster Hael (curved blades, halfling-hafted weapons, throwing axes)variable / 7-14 daysHael's specialty.
Armor repair / commissionMultiple Garden Quarter shops1-3 gp / 2-5 daysHalfling fitting available.
Spell-component refresh (any grade)Iren Velash (Selvara only; via referral, others)1-5 gp + components at cost / 1-2 daysIren stocks more variety than any other campaign location.
Identifying a magic item (any grade)Iren Velash or via the Greyhawk warlock fellowship (Alicia)10-50 gp / 1-3 daysThe campaign's deepest identification service. Very rare or unique items may take a week.
Pact-related item analysisThe Greyhawk warlock fellowship (Alicia only, by introduction)by donation / 1 dayThe fellowship is small but knowledgeable.
Druidic / hedge-witch herbal preparationsBrisa Wood at the Ash Tree (Kitty free; others 1-2 gp)1-2 daysBrisa's specialty: Greyhawk-urban-adapted herbs.
Manuscript transcription / paleographic analysisIren Velash5-30 gp / 2-7 daysIren's primary commercial service.

Training

Greyhawk is the campaign's best training city for varied martial styles, exotic weapon proficiencies, faction-related skills, and any specialty Iren or the Greyhawk warlock fellowship can introduce.

Skill / ToolTrainerNotes
Common (any non-Common language)Foreign Quarter language tutors (multiple)Standard rate. The most varied selection of language teachers in the campaign.
Suloise (language)Iren VelashIren's specialty.
Halfling cant (slang/cipher)Skarrel (Cam only initially; Cam may teach others under Skarrel's tolerance)Greyhawk halfling cant is the campaign's broadest halfling cipher.
Exotic martial styleKell Marrow at the Gravestone (multiple, depending on which trainers are accepting students)Standard rate.
Pact-of-the-Blade (warlock theory)The Greyhawk warlock fellowship (Alicia only; introduction required)Standard rate. Less specialized than Veth-Avir's Rel Astra archive but still useful.
Insight (skill)Sergeant Vandros, Brisa Wood, Iren Velash, Kell MarrowGreyhawk has many quiet teachers of insight; choose by character affinity.
Persuasion (skill, faction-aware)Sergeant VandrosVandros's specialty: persuading factions to leave one alone.
Stealth / Sleight of Hand (Foreign Quarter style)Skarrel (Cam only initially)Greyhawk's Foreign Quarter is the campaign's best stealth-training environment.
Athletics (pugilist)Kell Marrow at the GravestoneStandard rate.

Recuperation

ServiceProviderCost / DayNotes
Foreign Quarter inn (modest)multiple3-6 gp / dayStandard recuperation.
Garden Quarter inn (high-end)multiple5-10 gp / dayExcellent recuperation. Three days fully restores.
The Garden Quarter bath-housesmultiple, public1-3 gp / half-dayAtmospheric and restorative. Standard recuperation if used over three half-days.
Iren Velash's back-room cotIren Velash (Selvara free; others by Iren's invitation)1-2 gp / dayQuiet, scholarly. Includes use of Iren's back-shelf.
The Ash Tree (camping in the small lot)Brisa's tolerance (Kitty free; others by her invitation)freeAtmospheric. Recuperator may wake with one Inspiration die after the third day, similar to Reedrush / Three-Tree Grove. Brisa makes a small herb-tea for departure.
The Gravestone's small back-roomKell Marrow (Elle free; others by his invitation)1 gp / dayQuiet between sparring matches; not specifically recuperative beyond standard.
Cure disease / restoration (clerical)Multiple Greyhawk temples (specialty varies; ask)50-200 gp / 1 dayGreyhawk's temple network is the campaign's broadest. Most ailments can be addressed; very rare ones may still require referral.

1. Cam — "Skarrel's Question" (rogue affinity, sandbox-to-investigation bridge)

Skarrel does not have a separate quest for Cam. Instead, Skarrel asks Cam to share what he learns about the Brass Crow's foreign-packet traffic during the party's Vector A investigation. The exchange is information for information: anything Cam shares, Skarrel will reciprocate with what his own network has noticed.

How it resolves: Cam visits Skarrel after each significant Vector A investigation beat. Persuasion DC 11 (Skarrel respects Cam already; the DC is low). Skarrel's reciprocation comes in three increments: after the first share, the names of the two Greyhawk halfling fences he trusts (in addition to himself); after the second, an introduction to a discreet Garden Quarter receiver who handles items the conspiracy might recognize; after the third, a short verbal report on which Foreign Quarter watchers have been more visible than usual this fortnight (no specific names; pattern only).

Reward: Skarrel becomes Cam's permanent Greyhawk anchor. In any future phase the campaign returns to Greyhawk, Cam may visit the Folded Cup and assume Skarrel's standing without re-establishing it.

2. Elle — "The Boy Who Relied on Reach" (monk affinity)

Kell Marrow has been training a young human pugilist named Den, eighteen years old, for six months. Den has hit a plateau; Kell suspects he is over-relying on his reach (which is considerable) and not learning the slower discipline of footwork. Kell would like Elle to spar a few rounds with Den — "a halfling teaches what reach hides."

Den is friendly and earnest; he is not afraid of Elle's halfling-ness. He is afraid of being the only pugilist at the Gravestone who cannot solve a problem with reach.

How it resolves: Elle spars three rounds with Den at the Gravestone. Athletics or Acrobatics DC 12 to read Den's footwork problem (it is obvious to any monk; the DC is checking for tact). The right answer is to spar slowly enough that Den has to use his feet, give him three small specific pieces of correction (where his lead foot lands; how his weight shifts on the cross; the angle of his shoulder when he attempts a hook), and let him win one of the three rounds cleanly so the lesson lands. The wrong answer is to demonstrate halfling speed; Den will fold further.

Reward: Kell grants Elle standing membership at the Gravestone. She may spar with any of his trainees, attend the public competitions free of charge, and use the small back-room. Kell mentions, late in the conversation, that he occasionally hears things from his trainees about the Foreign Quarter; Elle may visit any time she wants the rumor.

3. Kitty — "The Ash Tree's Eastern Side" (druid affinity)

Brisa Wood has noticed that the Ash Tree at the eastern edge of the Foreign Quarter has been losing leaves on its eastern side for three weeks. The cause is unclear. Brisa would like Kitty's reading.

The cause: a Foreign Quarter blacksmith two streets to the east has begun firing a new coal-and-tar fire that produces smoke the prevailing wind carries directly to the Ash Tree's eastern boughs. The smoke is not deadly to the tree; it is uncomfortable. The tree is dropping leaves to reduce its respiratory exposure.

How it resolves: Kitty walks the eastern side of the Ash Tree at first light (Survival or Nature DC 12 to read the leaf-drop pattern). Investigation DC 12 to trace the smoke back to the blacksmith. The right answer is to speak to the blacksmith — politely, without naming the cause as druidic — and propose a small adjustment to his fire (shifting the chimney one degree, or using a different additive in his coal mix). The blacksmith is reasonable; he will adjust if asked respectfully. The wrong answer is to confront him with druidic authority; he will be defensive and the smoke will continue.

Reward: Brisa teaches Kitty a single Greyhawk-urban small-rite for asking a city-tree to release smoke-stress. (One-shot, GM's discretion. Useful in any urban environment for the rest of the campaign.)

4. Gianni — "The Smuggling Pattern" (ranger affinity)

Sergeant Vandros has been watching a small smuggling route through the Foreign Quarter's eastern gate. The smugglers are moving small wrapped packages with weight inconsistent with normal contraband (food, weapons, spirits). Vandros would like Gianni's eye on the pattern, off the record.

The packages are pre-Suel manuscripts being moved out of Greyhawk to a private collector in Saltmarsh, by a route that avoids customs because the manuscripts' provenance is uncertain. The smugglers are not conspiracy-related; they are simply unwilling to pay Greyhawk's manuscript-export tax. The collector in Saltmarsh is a retired Aerdy noble who pays well and asks no questions.

How it resolves: Gianni rides the eastern gate's pattern (Investigation or Survival DC 12 to confirm the packages' weight and timing). Persuasion DC 13 to learn the route from a willing dock-worker (or higher with an unwilling one). The right answer is to confirm the route, identify the contents (Iren Velash will read a sample for free if asked), and brief Vandros that this is a tax-evasion case rather than something more serious. Vandros will close the route quietly; the smugglers will move to a different route within a week, but at least the customs office will know.

Reward: Vandros formally extends Gianni's standing at the Foreign Quarter watch station. She may enter at any hour, request a watch escort for any reasonable purpose, and consult the watch's incident log without supervision. Vandros also mentions, with a small private nod, that Iren Velash is a friend of his.

5. Selvara — "The Pre-Suel Bundle" (sorcerer affinity)

Iren Velash has acquired a small bundle of correspondence in pre-Suel script via an estate-clearance and does not have time to read it carefully. She will pay Selvara 15 gp for a written summary.

The correspondence is a series of family letters between two pre-Suel sorcerers — siblings — written approximately 1100 CY. The letters are not dramatic; they discuss the management of inherited sorcerous traits in their respective households, recommend small herbal remedies for migraines, and politely argue about whether their late mother's preferred sorcerous discipline should be passed on to a particular niece. There is nothing connecting them to the Phase 5 conspiracy or to the broader campaign. They are simply a quiet historical document.

How it resolves: Selvara reads the bundle over two to three days at Iren's back-shelf. Investigation DC 13 to read pre-Suel paleography accurately (Selvara's training under Magister Vaden in Rel Astra has made this achievable; without that training, the DC is 16 and the work takes longer). The right answer is a careful, modest summary that does not overclaim. The wrong answer is to invent connections to the campaign that are not in the text.

Reward: Iren pays the agreed 15 gp. She also extends Selvara's standing in her shop — Selvara may borrow up to two folio volumes at a time, with appropriate book-care discipline. Iren's small back-room is open to Selvara at any hour.
